meaning: n.[保龄球] 中央木柱,主脑人物
examples: AOL became the kingpin and along came the Internet.
But there is no kingpin on the "most wanted" posters.
So the most important person in a project or business is the kingpin.
Intel, the kingpin in microprocessor chips, has also announced a series of milestones in silicon photonics.
The sample of one brand sold by British supermarket kingpin Tesco was more than a quarter horse.
